behaviors.description: Static prompt-template lint rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "static prompt-template lint check rejects ... leakage copied from SKILL.md."
Slot B: Failure boundary is local lint-rule logic over template text.
Slot C: Lower than Unit is impossible; no cross-module or infra dependency is required.
Final level: Unit, based on Slot B and Slot C. Rejected Integration because behavior text is about a static lint check, not module handoff/runtime assembly.
behaviors.test_description: Call the lint function with template fixtures that include and exclude copied phase instructions from SKILL.md excerpts; assert reject/accept decisions and violation locations.

behaviors.description: Fully assembled prompt from the real harness (after interpolation/fixtures) contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures)."
Slot B: Failure appears at module boundaries (template + interpolator + fixture loader).
Slot C: Unit tests on template/lint alone miss leakage introduced during assembly.
Final level: Integration, based on Slot B and Slot C. Rejected System because no real OS/network/subprocess behavior is required beyond in-process harness wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt assembly pipeline with representative fixtures and interpolation variables, then assert the final rendered prompt has no forbidden phase-instruction snippets.

behaviors.description: Test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context, not hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules."
Slot B: Correctness depends on real model decisions conditioned by loaded skill context.
Slot C: Integration with stubbed model can pass even if real model behavior is actually driven by hard-coded prompt leakage.
Final level: Agentic, based on Slot B and Slot C. Rejected Workflow because the behavior describes one model-conditioned evaluation, not a multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the real harness and live model with user-style prompt while sourcing skill text from plugin files; assert behavioral outcome aligns with skill context and fails if only hard-coded prompt rules are present.

behaviors.description: Harness can run the same user-style prompt against degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing prompt text.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text."
Slot B: Boundary is harness orchestration (variant selection + file loading + invocation parameters).
Slot C: Unit tests of variant selector alone miss end-to-end wiring mistakes that alter prompt text or source selection.
Final level: Integration, based on Slot B and Slot C. Rejected Agentic because this behavior is about harness capability/invariance, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute harness twice (baseline skill, degraded/missing variant) with identical user prompt text and compare invocation inputs to confirm only skill source changes while prompt text remains unchanged.

behaviors.description: If relevant SKILL.md section is removed/degraded, behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt."
Slot B: Pass/fail depends on live model behavior changing with skill-context quality.
Slot C: Integration with mocked responses can be forced to fail/pass and cannot prove real regression sensitivity.
Final level: Agentic, based on Slot B and Slot C. Rejected Workflow because only one prompt-response judgment is required, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model behavioral test with baseline and degraded SKILL.md using identical prompt; assert baseline passes and degraded variant fails due to structural behavior difference.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before JSON parsing/extraction.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs."
Slot B: Boundary is pipeline ordering across model client, capture writer, and parser modules.
Slot C: Unit tests on parser or writer alone miss sequencing bugs between modules.
Final level: Integration, based on Slot B and Slot C. Rejected Agentic because assertion is about ordering/capture infrastructure, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run harness path that receives model text and verify artifact contains unparsed raw output, then confirm parser/extractor runs afterward (e.g., via timestamps/events or ordered hooks).

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural constraints (schema paths, role-specific placement), not only keyword checks.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints ... not only keyword presence checks."
Slot B: Failure boundary is assertion-rule logic on provided output objects/text.
Slot C: Lower than Unit cannot express this; cross-module wiring is not required to catch malformed structure cases.
Final level: Unit, based on Slot B and Slot C. Rejected Integration because behavior text targets assertion semantics, not harness integration.
behaviors.test_description: Test assertion helpers with crafted outputs: keyword-present-but-structurally-wrong should fail; structurally-correct outputs should pass.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that keyword-only checks miss.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "Under live model execution ... catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss."
Slot B: Correctness depends on real LLM output variability and structure quality.
Slot C: Integration with deterministic stubs can hide true regressions because output structure is pre-fixed.
Final level: Agentic, based on Slot B and Slot C. Rejected Workflow because this is a single live-evaluation step, not a chained multi-agentic pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run the live model on regression-prone prompts and assert structural validators fail cases that still contain expected keywords; compare with keyword-only checker to prove miss gap.
